observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Treasurer (i) to furnish the updated and validated RPTAR/Taxpayer's index card to Municipal Accountant at every beginning of the year for recording purposes that is sourced from reliable records, and (ii) to intensify and strategize tax collection efforts from tax payers such as deployment of demand letters, posting of notice of delinquency in conspicuous places and tapping the assistance of barangay officials for the tax campaign to reach out to a larger area; and (b) Municipal Accountant to establish RPT and SET receivables sourced from the list duly certifed by the Treasury Office at the beginning of the year

We reiterated our prior year recommendation and the LCE agreed to direct (a) the Inventory Committee to facilitate a one-time cleansing of PPE account balances through the implementation of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31,2020; (b) the Municipal Accountant (i) to maintain an updated lapsing schedule showing correct computation of depreciation expense that will reconcile with the amounts recorded and presented in the financial statements; and ii) exert more effort to secure the necessary documents and reports, or alternative sources, so that adequate depreciation of all depreciable PPE items can be provided; and (c) Management execute valid donation documents for accounting recognition purposes. We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to make the necessary adjustment to account the proper treatment of collection and liquidated damages.

We recommended and Management agreed to facilitate the immediate planning of projects, programs and activities that will warrant the optimal use of funds received by the Municipality for the benefits of its constituents. We recommended and (a) the Local Chief Executive agreed to provide Certification by Legal Officer that there are no pending case relative to the account, provide Certification by the responsible officials to the effect that there are no records /documents available to validate claim, and consider reverting the dormant balances; and (b) Management agreed to return the unused balance of fund transfers to the source agencies in the compliance with Section 6.7 of COA Circular No 94-013 dated December 13,1994.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to direct the (a) BAC and Accounting Office to submit the lacking supporting documents, as discussed, and ensure to the completeness of documentation before the processing of payments of claim/s by the contractor/s; and (b) Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) to comply with the provisions of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No.1 specifically in ensuring that the development projects to be funded out of the 20% DF are well- planned and procurement and implementation- ready.

We commended Management for the efficient collection performance and recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to (i) continue to comply and enforce the Local Revenue so that correct business tax will accrue for the benefit of the Municipality and (ii) require the submission of sworn statement of business gross sales/receipt and copy of income tax return to ensure that the basis declared by the business establishments are reliable and accurate; and (iii) examine the books of businesses to ascertain the correctness of assessments and collection of business tax.

We recommended and the Management agreed to continue with withholding and remitting deductions in accordance with the aforesaid guideline. However, we reiterated our previous year's recommendation and Management agreed that they should design a more effective approach that allows a timelier recording of transactions to facilitate the reconciliation between actual taxes withheld and remitted.

We recommended and the Management agreed to continue with withholding and remitting deductions in accordance with statutory regulations. However, we reiterated our recommendation and the LCE agreed to direct the (i) Human Resource and Management Office and Accounting Office to take the necessary measures to lessen or eliminate the over and under remittances of the balances of Due to GSIS, PAGIBIG and Phil Health accounts (ii) Municipal Accountant to immediately make all necessary adjustment or remit all premiums due, when the evidence so warrants.

We recommended and the Management agreed that (i) the Accounting and
Treasury Offices continue maintaining proper accounting of the Cash accounts and
monitoring the book and bank reconciling items, and (ii) the Accounting Office
consistently prepare and submit the monthly bank reconciliation statements.

We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed that the (a)
Municipal Treasurer (i) furnish the updated and validated RPTAR/ Taxpayer’s index
card to Municipal Accountant at every beginning of the year for recording purposes
that is sourced from a reliable records; and (ii) intensify and strategize tax collection
efforts from taxpayers such as deployment of demand letters, posting of notice of
delinquency in conspicuous places and tapping the assistance of the barangay officials
for tax campaign to reach out to a larger area; and (b) Municipal Accountant to
establish RPT and SET receivables sourced from the list duly certified by the
Treasury Office at the beginning of the year.

We recommended and the Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer agreed
to (i) maintain the Stock Cards, Semi-Expendable Property Cards, Supplies Ledger
Cards and Semi-Expendable Property Ledger Cards for all LGU inventories and
observe the periodic reconciliation of the said records to monitor agency assets
properly; (ii) conduct a complete semi-annual physical count of inventories and
submit the corresponding RPCI not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year
in accordance with Section 124 of the MNGAs for LGUs; (iii) maintain an SSMI and
RSPI to serve as a basis for preparing Journal Entries to record the supplies and
materials and semi-expendable properties issued; (iv) maintain a RegSPI to ensure
proper monitoring of semi-expendable properties and (v) maintain a WMR to
establish transparency regarding the legitimacy of replacements and disposal of waste
materials.

We reiterated our prior year recommendation and Management agreed that
the (a) Inventory Committee facilitate a one-time cleansing of PPE account balances
through the implementation of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020;
(b) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer maintain complete property
records for all PPE; (c) Municipal Accountant (i) maintain an updated lapsing
schedule showing correct computation of depreciation expense that will reconcile with
the amounts recorded and presented in the FS; and ii) exert more effort to secure the
necessary documents and reports, or alternative sources, so that adequate
depreciation of all depreciable PPE items can be provided; and (d) Management
execute valid donation documents for accounting recognition purposes.

We recommended and Management agreed that the (a) Municipal Accountant
recognize leave benefits quarterly as they are earned, rather than recording the entire
liability in a single year; (b) Municipal Budget Officer establish an Employee Benefits
Reserve Fund to fund for all the leave benefits owed to employees; and (c) Municipal
Human Resource Management Office to coordinate with the Municipal Accounting
Office and Municipal Budget Office to ensure adherence to Rule XVI of the Omnibus
Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order (EO) No. 292 on the yearly
accounting and funding of leave benefits.

We reiterate the previous years’ audit recommendation that the Management
consider the write-off of dormant accounts after exhausting all means to locate documents
pertaining to the dormant fund transfers in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005
dated December 19, 2016 which provides the guidelines and procedures for the write-off
of dormant accounts, which include unliquidated fund transfers. The Circular defines
Dormant Unliquidated Fund Transfer as advances granted by the source entity to
implementing entity for the implementation of programs/projects which remained non-
moving for ten years or more and where a settlement could no longer be ascertained.

We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed that the (a) concerned
offices prepare a schedule of the account certified by the Accountant and approved by
the Local Chief Executive, provide Certification by Legal Officer that there are no
pending cases relative to the account, provide Certification by the responsible officials to
the effect that there are no records/documents available to validate claim, and consider
reverting the dormant balances; and (b) Management return the unused balance of fund
transfers to the source agencies in compliance with Section 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-
013 dated December 13, 1994.

We recommended and Management agreed that the (a) Local Chief Executive
(LCE) continue upholding its mandate in promoting local development by
appropriating 20% of its annual NTA pursuant to the provisions of the DILG-DBM
JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 and JMC No. 01 dated November 04,
2020; (b) BAC together with the Technical Working Group (TWG), submit the lacking
supporting documents, as discussed, and ensure the completeness of documentation
before the processing of payment of claim/s by the contractor/s.

We recommended and the MDRRM Officer, in collaboration with MDRRMC
agreed to (a) ensure that the use of LDRRMF is maximized; (b) identify and
incorporate, under a separate caption in the LDRRMFIP the projects, programs, and
activities that will be charged against the unexpended LDRRMF from previous years;
and (c) update or revise the LDRRMFIP as necessary to effect any changes in the
PPAs during the year.

We commended Management for the effective collection performance and
reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer (i) continue to comply
and enforce the Local Revenue Code so that correct business tax will accrue for the
benefit of the Municipality; (ii) require the submission of a sworn statement of the
business’ gross sales/receipt and copy of income tax return, to ensure that the basis
declared by the business establishments are reliable and accurate; and (iii) examine
the books of businesses to ascertain the correctness of assessment and collection of
business tax.

We recommended and the GFPS agreed to (i) strive for the full
implementation of the identified and approved PPAs by utilizing judiciously the fund
set aside for said purposes; and (ii) inform the concerned DILG Office in writing
regarding the changes in the DILG-endorsed GPB to ensure that the actual results
conformed with established performance indicator and target.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed to i) continue
to observe the rules and regulations governing the withholding and remittance of taxes to
the BIR; and ii) design a more effective strategy that will allow a timelier recording of
transactions to facilitate the reconciliation between actual taxes withheld and remitted.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed that the (a)
HRMO and Accounting Office take the necessary measures to lessen or eliminate the
over and/or under remittances of the balances of Due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG, and
PhilHealth accounts; and (b) Municipal Accountant immediately make all necessary
adjustments or remit all premiums due, when the evidence so warrants.

We recommended and the (i) Accounting and Treasury Offices agreed to keep up with the proper accounting of the Cash accounts and monitoring of the book and bank reconciling items, and (ii) Accounting Office continuously prepare and submit the monthly bank reconciliation statement

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) comply with the provisions of COA-DBM-DILG -GCG-DND JC No. 2015-01 relevant to appropriation, entitlement, release, use and reporting of the CF'S by liquidating the CA for CF at year-end; and (ii) use the proper accounts in the recognition and liquidation of CA and make the necessary adjustments to account the proper recording of CA'S for CF.

We recommended and the GFPS agreed to ensure the complete implementations of activities indicated in the GPB to maximize the intervention in addressing gender issues in the Municipality and warrant the preparation and submission of the three-year GAD Agenda.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the MSWMB to (i) to prioritize the review/update of the SWMP to ensure its sustainability, viability and effectiveness; (ii) harmonize the SWMP with the annual budget of the Municipality of; and (iii) oversee the implementation and completion of programmed SWM projects and interventions to ensure that the intended outcomes are fully achieved.

balances of Due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth accounts, (ii) Municipal
Accountant to immediately make all necessary adjustments or remit all premiums
due, when the evidence so warrants.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant regularly reconcile the cashbook balance with the accounting records so that errors committed can be identified and appropriate adjustments in the records can be effected.
Moreover, we recommended that the:
(i) Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant a list of canceled checks and unreleased checks at the end of the year which will serve as a basis in preparing JEV for the restoration of cash and liability accounts; and
(ii) Municipal Accountant prepare a JEV restoring cash and liability accounts equivalent to the canceled and unreleased checks at the close of the year, and another JEV reversing the previous entries made to recognize the availability of the checks for release the following year.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure proper classification of Loans Payable in the SFPos by presenting the portion due to be settled within 12 months after the reporting date as Current Liabilities and the remaining portion as Non-Current Liabilities.

We recommended that the Management return the unused balance of fund transfers to the SAs in compliance with Section 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.
Further, we recommended that the LCE instruct the Accounting Office, Budget Officer, and other concerned Offices to exhaust all means to locate documents pertaining to the dormant fund transfers, determine whether the purposes can still be achieved, and consider the write-off of dormant accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the:
a) LDRRM Officer to prepare the LDRRMFIP in its prescribed format as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2012-002; and
b) Municipal Accountant to i) present the minimum required disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements as specified in COA Circular No. 2014-002; and ii) check and ensure the completeness and reliability of documents before effecting payment.

We recommended that the LSB formulate the required three-year program so that utilization of the SEF would be maximized through coordination with the Municipal and Provincial investment and development plans.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant and MBO determine the unutilized appropriations and cause the inclusion of the same in preparing the SEF annual budget.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
(i) indicate control numbers on the ARE/ICS issued for properties purchased using the SEF to ensure completeness of the same and for proper monitoring of assets and (ii) issue the same under the name of the particular or actual recipients.
We further recommended that the Municipality coordinate with the DepEd regarding the monitoring of accountabilities of DepEd employees who are being reassigned and/or terminating their employment with the said agency, for the proper return or turnover of properties issued to them.

We recommended that BAC, its Secretariat and concerned officials submit copies of contracts entered into by the Municipality and/or issued POs within five (5) days from execution thereof in consonance with COA Circular No. 2009-01 to avoid suspension of the transactions and the penalty prescribed under 4.1 of this Circular.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System prioritize adopting the HGDG and PIMME tools to measure the actual cost attributed to gender-responsiveness and know if the existing mechanism actually responds to the basic 5% requirement of the law.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management design a more effective strategy that will allow a timelier recording of transactions to facilitate the reconciliation between actual taxes withheld and remitted.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to create an Inventory Committee to facilitate a one-time cleansing of PPE account balances through the implementation of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

We reiterated our recommendation and Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year a certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible during the year which will serve as basis of the latter in setting-up the RPT Receivables at the beginning of the year.

We recommended and Management agreed that:

a.	the Local Chief Executive direct the MDRRMO to review the DRRM projects and activities that were not implemented in CY 2020 and program their execution, if still warranted by the present circumstances or program new PPAs that may effectively address the demands of the current situation so that calamity-issues sought to be addressed by the initiatives are given the necessary solutions; and 

b.	the concerned Municipal officials and personnel submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team and observe compliance with the above-cited provisions of GPPB Circular in the emergency procurement of goods.

We reiterated our recommendation and Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and Human Resource and Management Officer to seriously exert their best effort in retrieving all pertinent documents and analyze the subject accounts to establish the accuracy of the undocumented balances of the three accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the (i)  Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRMO) to prioritize the implementation of PPAs on COVID-19 mitigation and response to strengthen the capacity of the Municipality to reduce the risk of the continuous spread of COVID-19 at the municipal level and enhance preparedness and response capabilities at all times; and (ii) Bids and Award Committee (BAC), BAC Secretariat and Accounting Office to submit the lacking documents as enumerated, in compliance with the revised implementing rules and regulations (IRR) of R.A. No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the LCE (i) require the submission of lacking supporting documents/records/orders to the Audit Team by the Municipal Human Resource Management Office (HRMO) and other concerned offices; and (ii) require the 12 mentioned officials to refund the COVID-19 AHDP totaling ₱61,636.40, unless a certification from the DBM exempting them from the conditions on the grant of COVID-19 AHDP is submitted.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the (a) Municipal Treasurer to update the RPTARs/ Taxpayers’ Index Card and subsequently, prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year a certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible during the year which will serve as basis of the latter in setting-up the RPT Receivables at the beginning of the year; and (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) establish RPT and SET receivables sourced from the list duly certified by the Treasury Office at the beginning of the year; (ii) make the necessary adjustments to account the proper treatment of advance tax collections; and (iii) align the accounting procedures being adopted with the procedures provided by the MNGAS for LGUs with respect to recording of RPT transactions.

We recommended that the LCE direct (a) the Inventory Committee to facilitate a one-time cleansing of PPE account balances through the implementation of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; and (b) the Municipal Accountant i) to maintain an updated lapsing schedule showing correct computation of depreciation expense that will reconcile with the amounts recorded and presented in the financial statements; and ii) exert more effort to secure the necessary documents and reports so that adequate depreciation of all depreciable PPE items can be provided.

We recommended that the (i) Management continue the practice of ensuring optimal use of DF so that socio-economic projects that are beneficial to the agency’s constituents are timely implemented; and (ii) Municipal Engineer require the project contractors to observe the proper details to be provided on project signs including the update thereof in accordance with the project status.

We recommended that the LCE direct the (a) MDRRMO to i) prepare the LDRRMFIP in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-02; ii) prioritize the implementation of PPAs on disaster prevention, mitigation and preparedness to strengthen the capacity of the Municipality to reduce risk at the municipal level and enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities at all times; and iii) refrain from charging against LDRRMF expenses which are not in accordance with the fund’s intended purpose; (b) MSWDO to submit clear guidelines allowing the charging of economic shelter assistance against LDRRM for guidance in audit; and (c) BAC, BAC Secretariat and Accounting Office to submit the lacking documents as enumerated, in compliance with the revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the LCE direct the (i) Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and Municipal Engineer to continue their collaborative efforts in pursuing future LGSF-funded programs in accordance with the guidelines set by the DILG or the DBM, as the case may be; (ii) BAC and its Secretariat to submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents as enumerated and warrant the completeness of supporting documents that need to be prepared/submitted in the procurement stages of projects in accordance with the provisions of DILG MC 2019-71, DILG MC 2020-088 and R.A. No. 9184 and its Revised IRR; and (iii) Municipal Accountant to ensure that all necessary documents are attached to the DVs are present before making the payment/s.

We recommended that Management review and evaluate the strategies being adopted in handling the operations of the Economic Enterprises, especially the PGH, adopt cost-cutting measures and perform revenue-cost analysis to determine further steps to be undertaken to attain a sustainable operation.

We recommended that the LCE direct the (a) Municipal Accountant to (i) ensure that all disbursements are supported with complete documentation and that necessary data/information are provided before making the payment/s; and (ii) secure from the DepEd Accountant a certification confirming that all expenses chargeable against the SEF are net of the budgetary provision for the same or related item/s in the DepEd budget; (b) BAC and its Secretariat to comply with all the provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its Revised IRR in the procurement of materials including those charged against the SEF; and (c) Municipal Budget Officer to prepare the APP for SEF, moving forward.

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE require the MSWMB to (i) include pre-identified solid wastes interventions in the annual budget of the agency to ensure funding for their implementation; and (ii) monitor the implementation and completion of said interventions with the end in view of assuring that desired outputs and outcomes are fully achieved.

We recommended that the LCE direct the (a) MSWMB to (i) prepare an accomplishment report and Progress Reports in line with the SWM Plan; (ii) facilitate the re-conduct of IEC campaigns; and (iii) cause the existing MRFs to become operational in accordance with Section 32 of R.A. No. 9003; and (b) MENRO-designate and MPDC to design contingency plans in order to put back on track the faithful implementation of the 10-Year SWM Plan.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the members of the GFPS Executive Committee and Technical Working Group (TWG) to (i) inform the concerned DILG Office, in writing, if there are changes in the DILG-endorsed GPB and/or if there is a need to implement additional projects and activities relevant to current gender issues or GAD related undertakings; (ii) enhance the GAD AR by including relevant documents that contain information needed to properly evaluate the implementation of GAD PPAs; (iii) equip themselves with the HGDG tool and PIMME to determine how much of the agency's actual disbursements for implementing its major programs can be attributed to GAD; and (iv) consider utilizing all available resources to update the GAD Database.

We recommended that Management i) design a more effective strategy that will allow a timelier recording of transactions to facilitate the reconciliation between actual taxes withheld and remitted; and ii) continue to observe the rules and regulations governing the withholding and remittance of taxes to the BIR.

We recommended that Management continue to withhold and remit loan amortization and mandatory contributions to the GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth. We further reiterated our previous audit recommendation that the Management exert extra effort in gathering pertinent documents and analyze the subject accounts to establish the accuracy of the balances in the inter-agency payables.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct all concerned agency officials and personnel to submit immediately the lacking documents to the Audit Team and strictly observe the provisions of the abovementioned regulations.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the concerned Municipal officials and employees to prepare all the required reports in a timely manner and submit the same to the concerned oversight government agencies to foster compliance to existing rules and promote transparency and accountability in the administration of donations intended to augment the needs of the constituents during the lockdown/quarantine period.

We recommended and Management agreed, henceforth, to improve the distribution of relief goods to recipients during emergencies and other calamities to fully serve the intended purpose. Moreover, the distribution list shall contain vital data as to the quantity of items issued and received for every phase/period of activity.

We recommended and Management agreed that all concerned agency officials and personnel submit immediately the lacking documents to the Audit Team and comply with the provisions of the abovementioned regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed to 

(a) continue the practice of ensuring optimal use of development funds so that socio-economic projects that are beneficial to the agency’s constituents are timely implemented; 

(b) give priority on the proper planning and implementation of PPAs from its continuing appropriation so that expected outputs and outcomes will be realized in the coming fiscal year; and 

(c) Bids and Awards Committee to strictly adhere to the above-cited provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its RIRR in the procurement activities.
We recommended and Management agreed to continue to observe the rules and regulations governing the withholding and remittance of taxes to the BIR.
We recommended and Management agreed to continue to exert efforts to enforce settlement of all suspensions and disallowances as required under COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

observations.reason: Due to unexpected constraints during the year, the Management was not able to fully execute all audit recommendations pertaining to PPE thus, they were reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation and Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year a certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible during the year which will serve as basis of the latter in setting-up the RPT Receivables at the beginning of the year.

Also, we recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant prepare the corresponding subsidiary ledgers (SLs) to monitor tax collections and delinquencies pursuant to Section 4, Chapter 2 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volume I.

We reiterated our recommendation and Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and Human Resource and Management Officer to seriously exert their best effort in retrieving all pertinent documents and analyze the subject accounts to establish the accuracy of the undocumented balances of the three accounts.
